title: National Day for Truth and Reconciliation), An Act to amend the Bills of Exchange Act, the Interpretation Act and the Canada Labour Code ( [Not in force]
title_en: National Day for Truth and Reconciliation), An Act to amend the Bills of Exchange Act, the Interpretation Act and the Canada Labour Code ( [Not in force]
short_name: National Day for Truth and Reconcilia...
description: An Act to amend the Bills of Exchange Act, the Interpretation Act and the Canada Labour Code (National Day for Truth and Reconciliation) (National Day for Truth and Reconcilia...). Current to 2026-02-04.

provisions.title: Purpose
provisions.content: 1 The purpose of this Act is to respond to the Truth and Reconciliation Commission of Canada’s call to action number 80 by creating a holiday called the National Day for Truth and Reconciliation, which seeks to honour First Nations, Inuit and Métis Survivors and their families and communities and to ensure that public commemoration of their history and the legacy of residential schools remains a vital component of the reconciliation process.

provisions.chapter: R.S., c. B-4 Bills of Exchange Act
provisions.title: Purpose

provisions.chapter: R.S., c. L-2 Canada Labour Code
provisions.title: Purpose
Two months after royal assent
provisions.content: 6 This Act comes into force on the day that, in the second month after the month in which it receives royal assent, has the same calendar number as the day on which it receives royal assent or, if that second month has no day with that number, the last day of that second month.
